strategy: He is immune to damage: every hit deals 0 but makes him self-damage 1. Chip him down with cheap multi-hit spells (Hydra's three hits do 3).
At 1 health he polymorphs into a 56,000-health form with no immunity, or use a multi-hitting spell like Hydra at low health to skip the polymorph and finish him outright.

The Hired Hoof minions each punish a specific action: auras (Supernova), heals (Infection), blades (Steal Charm), wards (Steal Ward), DoTs (Mass Triage), traps (Cleanse Ward), weakness (Cleanse Charm). Keep your setup narrow.
Once in the 56,000 form (or skipping it), blade up and hit for real damage.
Everyone join Round 1; latecomers get natural-attacked and stunned two rounds.
cheatNotes: Late joiners get natural-attacked and Stunned for two rounds, so join on Round 1.
He is immune to damage: every hit deals 0 but makes him self-damage 1, so use cheap multi-hit spells to chip him.
Multi-hit spells like Hydra trigger the self-damage once per hit, so they whittle him faster.
Damage-over-time does not trigger the self-damage, so DoTs will not chip him.
At 1 health he becomes a 56,000-health form that loses the damage immunity, so plan for a real damage race or skip it.
Casting a multi-hit spell like Hydra at low health can skip the polymorph and defeat him outright.
His natural attack is followed by a two-round Shadow Stun.
He summons Hired Hoof minions on Round 1 and every 6 rounds; each punishes a different action.
Casting an aura makes the Balance Hoof Supernova you, so skip auras.
Pet may-casts also trigger the aura Supernova.
Healing makes the Death Hoof Infect the caster.
Blading makes the Fire Hoof Steal Charm.
Warding makes the Ice Hoof Steal Ward (from the caster on Legion Shield or Rebirth).
DoTs make the Life Hoof Mass Triage them off.
Trapping makes the Myth Hoof Cleanse Ward.
Weakness makes the Storm Hoof Cleanse Charm it off.
